groupDefinition.name: Low-Level Inspection
groupDefinition.description: Advanced tools for memory viewing, register analysis, and assembly code inspection.

items.title: Integration of Tabbed Layout and Navigation
items.description: Evolve the main content area into a **tabbed interface** and implement logic for dynamic switching between Source and Disassembly.
details: Replace static Monaco Editor with a `mat-tab-group` containing Source and Disassembly tabs.
Implement active tab management in `DebuggerComponent`.
Add logic to automatically switch to Disassembly tab if a selected stack frame has no source but has an instruction pointer.

items.title: DapAssemblyService and Disassemble Request
items.description: Implement a dedicated service for assembly data retrieval and interaction with the DAP `disassemble` endpoint.
details: Create `DapAssemblyService` with a component-scoped lifecycle.
Implement the `disassemble` request with configurable instruction count and offsets.
Handle symbol resolution and address mapping within the service.
Expose a reactive `instructions$` stream for the UI.
[Test] Verify disassemble request serialization with correct memory address and instruction offset
[Test] Verify instructions$ emits and updates on new disassembly data
[Test] Verify error handling when disassemble request fails (e.g., restricted memory range)

items.title: AssemblyViewComponent and Instruction Rendering
items.description: Build the high-density instruction list component with **virtual scrolling** and instruction pointer highlighting.
details: Implement `AssemblyViewComponent` using `cdk-virtual-scroll-viewport`.
Design the high-density table layout (Address, Opcode, Mnemonic, Annotation).
Implement logic to synchronize the highlighted instruction with the current `instructionPointerReference`.
Integrate breakpoint markers in the assembly gutter.
[Test] Verify virtual scrolling correctly renders a subset of the assembly instructions and handles rapid scrolling.
[Test] Verify the highlighted instruction is synchronized with the current instruction pointer reference.
[Test] Verify breakpoint markers are accurately mapped to the corresponding address rows.

items.title: Implement Instruction-Level Stepping (stepi/nexti)
items.description: Support granular execution control at the instruction level with auto-view switching and strong DAP typing.
details: [dap.types.ts] Add `SteppingGranularity` union type ('statement' | 'line' | 'instruction') and `StepArguments` interface to enforce strong-typed DAP step requests.

[DapSessionService] Implement `nextInstruction()`: sends `next` request with `{ threadId: 1, granularity: 'instruction' }`. No capability guard — silent degradation via existing `_dapError` synthetic event on unsupported adapters.
[DapSessionService] Implement `stepInInstruction()`: sends `stepIn` request with `{ threadId: 1, granularity: 'instruction' }`. Same silent-degradation policy.

[DebugControlGroupComponent] Add two new icon buttons (stepi/nexti) to the 'Stepping' segment with Option B visual weight: reduced opacity/secondary style when active tab is Source; full primary weight when Disassembly tab is active.

[DebugControlGroupComponent] Add `@Output() stepInstructionTab = new EventEmitter<'stepi' | 'nexti'>()` matching the existing `restart`/`stop` @Output pattern. The emitted value signals which instruction-step action was requested.
[DebuggerComponent] Subscribe to `stepInstructionTab` output: set `activeTabIndex = 1` (Disassembly), then invoke the corresponding `DapSessionService` method.
[Test] Verify `nextInstruction()` serializes DAP request with `granularity: 'instruction'` and correct `threadId`.
[Test] Verify `stepInInstruction()` serializes DAP request with `granularity: 'instruction'` and correct `threadId`.
[Test] Verify stepi/nexti buttons are disabled when executionState !== 'stopped'.
[Test] Verify `stepInstructionTab` EventEmitter emits and DebuggerComponent switches to Disassembly tab (activeTabIndex = 1).

items.title: Library Extraction: AssemblyViewComponent
items.description: Extract the AssemblyViewComponent into a dedicated library @taro/ui-assembly to isolate specialized disassembly rendering logic.
details: Create projects/ui-assembly library
Relocate assembly-view.component.ts and associated styles/templates to the library
Ensure virtual-scroll and DAP type dependencies are correctly handled
Update DebuggerComponent to consume from @taro/ui-assembly
[Test] Verify disassembly view renders and highlights correctly
[Doc] docs/archive/specs/assembly-view-spec.md

items.title: Implement Memory View (Hex Dump)
items.description: Add a low-level memory inspection interface to the center tab group.
details: 1. Update dap.types.ts with readMemory/writeMemory schemas.
2. Implement DapMemoryService.
3. Create MemoryViewComponent.
4. Integrate into DebuggerComponent tabs and Variables context menu.
[Test] Verify memory data matches hex dump after 'readMemory' success.
[Doc] docs/archive/specs/memory-view-spec.md

items.title: DAP Memory Protocol & Service
items.description: Define readMemory/writeMemory types and implement DapMemoryService in dap-core.
details: Update dap.types.ts with readMemory/writeMemory schemas
Implement DapMemoryService with Base64/Hex conversion
Handle memoryReference resolution
[Test] Unit test DapMemoryService with mock DAP session
[Doc] docs/archive/specs/memory-view-spec.md

items.title: Memory Hex Dump Component
items.description: Create the standalone MemoryViewComponent with hex/ASCII rendering.
details: Create MemoryViewComponent in ui-inspection library
Implement hex grid with cdk-virtual-scroll
Implement ASCII character preview panel
[Test] Verify hex alignment and virtual scroll performance with large buffers
[Doc] docs/archive/specs/memory-view-spec.md

items.title: Memory View Host Integration
items.description: Integrate Memory View into the center tabs and Variables context menu.
details: Add 'Memory' tab to DebuggerComponent center panel
Implement 'Inspect Memory' context menu in Variables tree
Wire address bar navigation and manual input
[Test] Verify end-to-end flow from Variables right-click to Memory view render
[Doc] docs/archive/specs/memory-view-spec.md

items.title: DapRegisterService Implementation
items.description: Create a reactive service to manage register states and identify changes.
details: Implement periodic/event-driven fetching of the 'Registers' scope
Implement a 'diff' mechanism to track value changes between stopped events
Expose registers$: Observable<RegisterState[]>
[Doc] docs/archive/specs/assembly-registers-integration.md
[Test] Unit test DapRegisterService with mock DAP session

items.title: AssemblyRegisterPanelComponent
items.description: Build the high-density UI component for the register list with mutation highlights.
details: Create AssemblyRegisterPanelComponent in ui-assembly library
Implement conditional styling for 'changed' values
Add format toggles (Hex/Dec/Bin) via context menu
[Doc] docs/archive/specs/assembly-registers-integration.md
[Test] Verify register mutation highlights in Storybook/Mock context

items.title: Assembly Dashboard Integration
items.description: Integrate the Register Panel into the Assembly View with a resizable split-pane.
details: Refactor AssemblyViewComponent to use a taro-panel-group layout
Integrate AssemblyRegisterPanelComponent as a nested child
Implement a toggle button to show/hide the register panel
[Doc] docs/archive/specs/assembly-registers-integration.md
[Test] Verify resizable split layout and panel visibility toggling

items.title: Assembly Instruction Cache Implementation
items.description: Implement a memory-resident instruction cache for the disassembly view to improve scrolling performance and reduce redundant DAP requests.
details: [Architecture] Design address-range based cache indexing using BigInt
[Implementation] Modify DapAssemblyService to check cache before dispatching disassemble command
[Logic] Implement instruction merging, Spatial Pruning, and Auto-fetch for infinite scrolling
[Persistence] Clear cache on debug session termination or active thread change
[Test] Verify that repeated scrolling to the same address range does not trigger new DAP requests
[Doc] docs/archive/specs/assembly-cache-spec.md

items.title: Decouple Disassembly Cache to Core
items.description: Migrate disassembly caching and enhancement logic from ui-assembly to dap-core to enable reuse and architectural alignment.
details: [Refactor] Extract DapAssemblyCacheService to dap-core/session
[Refactor] Migrate TaroDisassembledInstruction type to dap-core
[UI] Update ui-assembly service to delegate caching to the new core service
[Test] Verify caching and pruning logic with Vitest
[Doc] docs/archive/specs/assembly-core-migration.md

items.title: Centralize Assembly PC & Window Logic
items.description: Migrate Program Counter (PC) state and centered instruction window logic (+/- 1000) to DapAssemblyService.
details: Add currentPc$ and setPC() to DapAssemblyService
Implement centered window fetching (+/- 1000) in DapAssemblyService
Update AssemblyViewComponent to bind to service PC state
Update DebuggerComponent to update assembly PC on frame selection
[Test] Verify assembly view automatically centers on PC after stepping
[Test] Verify assembly view maintains +/- 1000 instruction context after frame switching
[Doc] docs/archive/specs/assembly-pc-centralization.md

items.title: Assembly View: Address-Based Navigation
items.description: Replace the inaccurate virtual scrollbar in the Assembly View with a hidden scrollbar and a Jump-to-Address toolbar input, eliminating the misleading scrollbar thumb caused by the unknown instruction address space.
details: Remove vertical scrollbar from cdk-virtual-scroll-viewport via CSS
Add a new pin_drop mat-mini-fab button to .floating-actions, positioned above the Return-to-PC FAB
Create standalone JumpToAddressDialogComponent in ui-assembly with hex input, inline validation, Cancel and Jump actions
Wire dialog afterClosed() result in AssemblyViewComponent to call relocateWindow() with normalized address
Retain the existing Return-to-PC floating action button as the primary escape hatch
[Test] Verify that clicking the Jump FAB opens JumpToAddressDialogComponent via MatDialog
[Test] Verify that a confirmed valid hex address calls relocateWindow() with the correct normalized address
[Test] Verify that invalid input shows mat-error and does not close the dialog or call relocateWindow()
[Test] Verify that the scrollbar is not visible and no layout regression occurs
[Doc] docs/archive/specs/assembly-address-navigation.md

items.title: Decouple viewport from execution PC in Assembly View
items.description: Introduce viewAnchor state to preserve manual navigation and improve reactive rendering.
details: Introduce viewAnchor WritableSignal
Refactor ResizeObserver becameVisible to use viewAnchor
Update JumpToAddressDialog to update viewAnchor
Sync currentPc to viewAnchor on break/step
[Test] Verify Jump to Address does not reset to PC on tab switch
[Doc] docs/archive/specs/assembly-view-anchor-spec.md

items.title: Assembly View: Fix Opcode and Mnemonic Column Truncation
items.description: Resolve the silent truncation of instruction bytes (opcode column) and instruction text (mnemonic column) in the Assembly View by replacing fixed-width clipping with flexible, tooltip-assisted rendering.
details: Audit opcode column width against max real-world x86/ARM byte sequences
Audit mnemonic column width against long operand patterns (e.g. memory-indirect addressing)
Replace fixed flex-basis with min-width constraints and allow columns to expand
Add text-overflow: ellipsis with title attribute tooltips on opcode and mnemonic cells
Ensure row layout remains single-line (white-space: nowrap preserved)
[Test] Verify that a 6-byte opcode (e.g. FF 25 00 00 00 00) is fully visible at standard viewport width
[Test] Verify that a long mnemonic (e.g. movq -0x8(%rbp), %rax) is not clipped in a 1280px viewport
[Test] Verify tooltip (title attr) appears on hover for truncated cells
[Doc] docs/archive/specs/assembly-column-truncation-fix.md

items.title: Remove sortedAddresses from DapAssemblyCacheService
items.description: Refactor the assembly instruction cache to eliminate the redundant sortedAddresses array by embedding instruction arrays directly within each CachedRange, reducing merge complexity from O(N log N) to O(K+M).
details: Embed instructions: bigint[] in CachedRange to replace global Map + sortedAddresses
Rewrite getFromCache to binary-search within the range-local instruction array
Remove updateSortedAddresses() and replace with range-local insertion
Update evictRange() to drop the CachedRange object entirely
Update pruneCache() to operate on CachedRange array only
[Test] Verify all existing cache spec tests pass with new storage model
[Test] Verify pruning evicts the correct range and sortedAddresses no longer exists
[Doc] docs/archive/specs/assembly-cache-range-refactor.md

items.title: Fix Assembly View Icon Flashing during Stepping
items.description: Prevent floating action icons in the Assembly View from flashing by replacing conditional rendering with persistent elements and reactive state-based disabling.
details: Remove *ngIf='currentPc()' from AssemblyViewComponent template
Implement [disabled] logic for 'Reveal PC' button based on PC availability
[Test] Verify icons remain stable (no DOM unmounting) during rapid 'step-in' operations

items.title: Fix Assembly Header Symbol Overflow
items.description: Resolve vertical overflow and overlapping text in the sticky function header caused by long C++ symbols.
details: AC-1: Relocate CppSignaturePipe to ui-shared. AC-2: Apply pipe to assembly header. AC-3: Single-line enforcement with ellipsis. AC-4: Full mangled name in tooltip.

items.title: Memory Layout Visualization & Probing
items.description: Implement the 'Advanced UX' features from Section 7 of the memory-view-spec.md, including struct layout overlays and member probing.
details: Implement member probing strategy in DapMemoryService using evaluate requests
Contextual Inspect Memory Layout action in Variables menu to load struct dimensions
Struct member byte cells shaded using dedicated HSL colors matching offsets
Alignment gaps visually hatched and labeled as [padding]
Floating labels displaying member names rendered above starting cell
[Doc] docs/archive/specs/memory-view-spec.md
[Test] Verify struct layout visualization for a nested C++ object

items.title: Inline Memory Editing Support
items.description: Enable users to modify memory contents directly from the Hex Dump interface.
details: Double-clicking a byte cell loads a text input with strict two-character hexadecimal formatting
Pressing Enter or clicking outside triggers a DAP writeMemory event
Successful modifications display a temporary green highlight transition
Failed modifications rollback instantly, display original data, and highlight error with red border
[Doc] docs/archive/specs/memory-view-spec.md
[Test] Verify memory write transaction flow and rollback on failure

items.title: Assembly View: Optimize Cache Hits & DAP Communication Efficiency
items.description: Optimize disassembly caching logic and thread stopped queries to eliminate redundant DAP requests, prevent cache-miss loops, and improve stepping performance.
details: Optimize address range calculation in DapAssemblyCacheService to prevent immediate cache eviction during stepping
Implement partial cache hit logic in getFromCache to retrieve only available instruction ranges instead of returning empty array
Reduce ASSEMBLY_WINDOW_SIZE in AssemblyViewComponent to a reasonable viewport size to minimize transfer payload
Consolidate duplicate thread stopped queries using dynamic multicast or RxJS shareReplay in DapSessionService
[Doc] docs/archive/specs/assembly-communication-efficiency.md
[Test] Verify partial cache hit returns slice from correct range when boundaries exceed cached range
[Test] Verify optimized guessBytes formula and safe count margin calculation for negative offset disassemblies
[Test] Verify gap-filling for prefix misses by fetching only the missing address slice
[Test] Verify concurrent thread queries coalesce into a single in-flight DAP command

items.title: Redefine Assembly View Header & Symbol Extraction
items.description: Remove sticky header, implement inline function labels, range-level symbol propagation, and GDB info symbol fallback.
details: Remove sticky header from HTML/CSS
Render inline function label when instruction.isFunctionStart is true
Update DapAssemblyCacheService for range-level symbol propagation
Implement GDB CLI fallback query via evaluate request
Refactor isOutOfRange to only dim zero padding and unmapped memory blocks
[Doc] docs/archive/specs/assembly-header-redefinition.md
[Test] Add unit tests in dap-assembly-cache.service.spec.ts

items.title: Infinite Memory View Scroll & Anchoring
items.description: Implement dynamic paging of memory blocks on-demand during scrolling with scroll anchoring loop prevention.
details: Implement bidirectional scroll trigger boundaries (THRESHOLD_ROWS = 10) in MemoryViewComponent
Implement Scroll Anchoring in MemoryViewComponent using offset delta to prevent jumps
Render greyed-out cell placeholders for unmapped memory pages
[Doc] docs/archive/specs/memory-view-spec.md
[Test] Verify scroll anchoring offset correction during prepend operations

items.title: Memory Segment Minimap
items.description: Implement a high-density vertical minimap track next to the memory view scrollbar to visualize and navigate virtual memory segments.
details: Create MemorySegmentMinimapComponent in ui-inspection library
Integrate segment indicators showing variable and stack frame positions in the track
Implement drag-to-scroll navigation within the target segment boundaries
[Doc] docs/archive/specs/memory-minimap-spec.md
[Test] Verify minimap coordinates translate scroll positions correctly in mock context
